title: Which Of The Following Is Not The Purpose Of Hipaa
source_filename: Which Of The Following Is Not The Purpose Of Hipaa.docx

qas.q: Define HIPAA
qas.a: HIPAA is the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996.

qas.q: A patient has the following rights
qas.a:
-Access, copy, inspect their PHI.
- Request Amendments
-Obtain accounting of most disclosures
-Receive communications from providers via other means
-Complain about alleged violations
-request restrictions on uses or disclosures

qas.q: PHI stands for
examples of PHI are
qas.a: Name, address, relatives and employer names, birth date, telephone numbers, social security number, medical record number, health plan beneficiary number, account number, certificate or license number, fax number, email address, serial number of any vehicle or other device, website address, fingerprints or voice prints, photographic images.

qas.q: What are the exceptions to disclosure standards in HIPAA?
qas.a: Court orders, workers' compensation cases, statutory reports, correctional institutions, national security, intelligence, incidental use and disclosure, or other essential government purpose.

qas.q: The ROI form is permitted for:
The term————means sharing PHI information outside of an entity.
What is considered a covered entity by HIPAA?
qas.a: Providers such as hospitals, nursing skilled facilities, critical access hospitals, home health agencies, pharmacies, hospices, comprehensive outpatient rehabilitation facilities, physician practices, dental practices, chiropractors, therapists, laboratories, podiatrists, and osteopaths.

qas.q: Who is responsible for HIPAA enforcement?
What are the main purposes of HIPAA?
qas.a: Increase healthcare efficiency and effectiveness. Protect and enhance patient rights. Restore trust in the healthcare system.

qas.q: Which of the following is not considered a covered entity by HIPPA?
All of the following are examples of PHI except:
The ROI form is permitted for all of the following except:
qas.a: Making an appointment with the provider

qas.q: Which of the following is NOT a main purpose of HIPAA?
qas.a: Establishing electronic medial records in the clinics and hospitals.

qas.q: All of the following are exceptions to disclosure standards except:
Define OSHA
What us the main purpose of OSHA?
qas.a: OSHA's responsibility is to improve worker safety and health protection

qas.q: What are the employee right according to OSHA
qas.a:
-A safe and healthful workplace
-Know about hazardous chemicals
-Report injury to employer
-Complain or Request hazard correction from employer
-Training
-Hazard exposure and medical records
-File a complaint with OSHA
-Participate in an OSHA inspection
-Be free from retaliation for exercising safety and health rights

qas.q: What is the mortality rate for workers
qas.a: 4,405 workers were killed on the job in 2013(3.2 per 100,000 full time equivalent workers

qas.q: Who is responsible for making sure the workplace is safe?
qas.a: It is the duty of employers to provide works places that are free of known dangers that could harm their employees

qas.q: What is a MSDS(orSDS)
qas.a: Safety Data Sheets (formerly known as Material Safety Data Sheets or MSDSs)

qas.q: What must be included in written hazard communication programs?
qas.a:
-Container labeling
-safety data sheets (SDSs)
-Worker Training
-The Training must include the physical and health hazards of chemicals and how workers can protect themselves
-communicate the hazards of hazardous chemical products

qas.q: In what instance could a worker file a confidential complaint with OSHA if the employee believes there is a violation of?
qas.a: If the employer believes there is unsafe or unhealthful conditions in the work place

qas.q: What are the standard groups of OSHA?
qas.a: General industry, construction, matrime, and agriculture

qas.q: What are the types of OSHA violations?
qas.a: Willful, serious, other than serious and repeated

qas.q: What is the difference between a willful violation and serious violation
qas.a: In a serious violation, the employer knew it could result in harm or death

qas.q: What is an other than serious violation?
qas.a: A violation that has a direct relationship to safety and health, but probably would not cause death or serious physical harm

qas.q: Workers may file a confidential complaint with OSHA if they believe there is a violation of any of the following except:
On average how many workers nearly die each day?
